original:
"Mundanians," he continues, "hold that Ludd's individual humanity provides a... spiritual focus. A manner of relation, a holy sympathy. Physical objects said to be associated with Ludd may play a role, hence the practice of veneration through - not of - relics. At least that is how they would describe such heresy. It is the apologia of Idolators."

"The ban on venerating the worldly individual of Ludd is clear, but the Word is less direct on the matter of proximal objects."

"We consider the conscious unknowing of Ludd's secular individuality a sign of devotion and a functional virtue."

He gives you a look. "Meaning we view the veneration of relics as idolatry. Some amongst us call the Mundanians 'Idolators' for their views. There are other... conflicts of practice, as well."

"We consider the conscious unknowing of Ludd's secular individuality a sign of devotion and a functional virtue. This is as the Word tells it."

He gives you a look. "Meaning we view the veneration of relics as idolatry. Some amongst us call the Mundanians 'Idolators' for their views, and not without justification."

"Ah." Bornanew looks a little embarrassed.

"They call us 'Ignorants'. For we choose to wallow in our own ignorance. From a certain point of view."

"And, ah," Bornanew's brow furrows. "Mind your use of such nomenclature. It would be inconvenient to cause offense at some inopportune moment."

"Besides, name-calling is unseemly for a Knight who ought hold himself to higher standards. I am sworn to face conflict directly by the truth of the path illuminated by faith."
"Best not to cast such words about thoughtlessly."

Bornanew halts for a moment, his breathing heavy.

"I beg you spare a moment, captain," he says self-consciously. "It... has taken much of me."

Bornanew halts for a moment, his breathing heavy.

"I beg you spare a moment, captain," he says self-consciously. "It... has taken much of me."

"You were saying... boring, yes. I suppose it is," he says diplomatically.

"I shan't belabor the point. I tell you this now because there may be opposition to my investigation, and to my arrest of Cedra Keepfaith."

He takes one more deep breath, then replies calmly, eyes on the path ahead.

"There was... passive opposition to my investigation. I intend to arrest Subcurate Cedra Keepfaith. Opposition may become... less passive."
